binaries.title: mup.sys - WIL feature-state cache library churn (bit 0x40000 marking + feature-usage provider registration)

changed_functions.name: wil_details_RegisterFeatureUsageProvider
key_changes: Added two stack locals in the record passed to RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider: one initialized to 1, one initialized to 0
No change to memory-safety or request-handling logic
changed_functions.security_notes: WIL feature-usage telemetry provider registration. Adjusts the registration record passed to RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider. Not security-relevant.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState
key_changes: PATCHED: Added 'bts eax, 12h' at 0x1400031DC, unconditionally setting bit 18 (0x40000) of the recomputed WIL feature-enabled-state cache value at the top of each CAS iteration
PATCHED: The conditional merge mask changed from '& 0xFFFFF63E | 2' to '& 0xFFFBF63E | 0x40000 | 2', keeping bit 18 set on that branch as well
PATCHED: Removed the redundant early stack store previously at 0x1400031E9 (mov dword ptr [rsp+arg_8], eax)
The interlocked commit at 0x140003229 (lock cmpxchg [r15], esi) is unchanged
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a Windows Implementation Library (WIL) feature-staging function, not a UNC prefix cache. It recomputes WIL's cached feature-enabled-state value for a compile-time feature gate and commits it with _InterlockedCompareExchange. The patched build always sets bit 0x40000 in the recomputed value; that bit is internal to WIL's feature-state cache encoding and is consumed only by WIL's own IsEnabled checks. The function is reached only from wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (0x140002C3C) and wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (0x1400033FC), which evaluate compile-time WIL feature descriptors (e.g. Feature_364057912__private_featureState). It is NOT reachable from IRP_MJ_CREATE, a UNC path parser, or any attacker-controlled input, and the state field is a feature gate with no managed alloc/free object behind it. There is no use-after-free, no race with security impact, and no exploit primitive. This is statically-linked library servicing churn.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (changed region) ===
00000001400031D8  mov     eax, edi
00000001400031DA  mov     ecx, edi
00000001400031DC  cmp     [rsp+48h+arg_0], 0
00000001400031E1  mov     esi, eax
00000001400031E3  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax
00000001400031E7  jz      short loc_140003208
00000001400031E9  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax   ; redundant early store
00000001400031ED  test    cl, 2
00000001400031F0  jnz     short loc_140003208
00000001400031F2  and     esi, 0FFFFF63Eh
00000001400031F8  mov     eax, ebx
00000001400031FA  and     eax, 9C1h
00000001400031FF  or      esi, eax
0000000140003201  or      esi, 2
0000000140003204  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], esi
0000000140003227  mov     eax, edi
0000000140003229  lock cmpxchg [r15], esi
0000000140003234  jmp     short loc_1400031DC   ; retry loop

=== PATCHED wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (changed region) ===
00000001400031D8  mov     eax, edi
00000001400031DA  mov     ecx, edi
00000001400031DC  bts     eax, 12h              ; NEW: force bit 0x40000 each iteration
00000001400031E0  cmp     [rsp+48h+arg_0], 0
00000001400031E5  mov     esi, eax
00000001400031E7  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], eax
00000001400031EB  jz      short loc_140003208
00000001400031ED  test    cl, 2                 ; redundant early store removed
00000001400031F0  jnz     short loc_140003208
00000001400031F2  and     esi, 0FFFFF63Eh
00000001400031F8  mov     eax, ebx
00000001400031FA  and     eax, 9C1h
00000001400031FF  or      esi, eax
0000000140003201  or      esi, 2
0000000140003204  mov     dword ptr [rsp+48h+arg_8], esi
0000000140003227  mov     eax, edi
0000000140003229  lock cmpxchg [r15], esi
0000000140003234  jmp     short loc_1400031DC   ; retry loop (bts re-runs each iteration)

security_findings.entry_point: Not attacker-reachable. Called only by WIL feature-gate evaluation helpers wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (0x140002C3C) and wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (0x1400033FC), which evaluate compile-time feature descriptors.
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None. WIL feature-staging library behavior refinement.
security_findings.description: The function at 0x140003180 is wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState, part of the statically-linked Windows Implementation Library (WIL) feature-management code. It recomputes WIL's cached 'feature enabled state' packed value for a compile-time feature gate and commits it with an _InterlockedCompareExchange (lock cmpxchg) retry loop. The patched build unconditionally sets bit 18 (0x40000) of the recomputed value on every CAS iteration (bts eax, 12h at 0x1400031DC), where the unpatched build carried that bit only on the merge branch; the conditional mask was adjusted from & 0xFFFFF63E to & 0xFFFBF63E | 0x40000 accordingly, and a redundant early stack store was removed. This bit is internal to WIL's feature-state cache encoding and is consumed only by WIL's own IsEnabled checks. The state pointer is a compile-time WIL feature descriptor (e.g. Feature_364057912__private_featureState), not a per-request object. There is no path from IRP_MJ_CREATE on \Device\Mup or from a UNC path parser into this function, no attacker-influenced state field, no managed alloc/free object behind the state, and therefore no use-after-free, no race with security impact, and no exploit primitive. This is library servicing churn.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. The feature-state cache is driven by feature-staging configuration, not by attacker-controlled file I/O. The function is not reachable from a UNC path open or any IRP field.

breakpoints.why: Entry to the WIL feature-state cache reevaluation function. RCX (a1) = pointer to the compile-time WIL feature-state field being updated. RDX (a2) = current packed state. R8 (a3) = WIL feature descriptor. This is a feature-gate cache updater, not a request-handling path.
The single instruction-level delta. UNPATCHED: 'cmp [rsp+arg_0], 0'. PATCHED: 'bts eax, 12h', forcing bit 0x40000 of the recomputed WIL cache value each CAS iteration.
lock cmpxchg [r15], esi — the interlocked commit of the recomputed WIL feature-cache value. Unchanged between builds. R15 points to the compile-time feature-state field.

watch: ESI at 0x140003229 — value committed by lock cmpxchg; in the patched build it always carries bit 0x40000
R15 — pointer to the WIL compile-time feature-state field (not a per-request object)
R8 / a3 — WIL feature descriptor passed by the caller (e.g. Feature_364057912__private_featureState)
key_offsets: 0x1400031DC: UNPATCHED 'cmp [rsp+arg_0], 0'; PATCHED 'bts eax, 12h' — the added instruction that forces bit 0x40000. This is the whole behavioral delta.
0x1400031E9: UNPATCHED redundant early store 'mov dword ptr [rsp+arg_8], eax' — removed in the patched build.
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Not applicable. The WIL feature-state cache is driven by feature-staging configuration; it is not reachable from a UNC path open or any attacker-controlled IRP field.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the patched build the committed WIL feature-cache value carries bit 0x40000. This is a library-internal encoding flag consumed only by WIL's own IsEnabled checks; it has no security consequence.
debugging_aids.notes: The function at RVA 0x3180 is wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState in both builds. It calls wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState, g_wil_details_ensureSubscribedToFeatureConfigurationChanges, and g_wil_details_subscribeFeatureStateCacheToConfigurationChanges — all WIL feature-staging internals. Callers are wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (0x140002C3C) and wil_details_IsEnabledFallback (0x1400033FC). This is not a UNC prefix cache and not attacker-reachable.

summary: The mup.sys patch contains no security-relevant change. Two functions have behavioral body changes and both are inside the statically-linked Windows Implementation Library (WIL) feature-staging code. wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (0x140003180) now unconditionally sets bit 0x40000 in the recomputed WIL feature-enabled-state cache value before its interlocked commit (bts eax, 12h at 0x1400031DC) and drops a redundant early store; this bit is a WIL cache-encoding flag consumed only by WIL's own IsEnabled checks, the state field is a compile-time feature gate, and the function is reached only from WIL feature-gate helpers (0x140002C3C, 0x1400033FC), not from IRP_MJ_CREATE or any UNC path parser. wil_details_RegisterFeatureUsageProvider (0x14001238C) adds two locals to the record passed to RtlRegisterFeatureUsageProvider (WIL feature-usage telemetry registration). A third difference is a WPP trace-stub symbol rename (WPP_SF_d to WPP_SF_D) with an identical body. None of these are memory-safety, race, or privilege-escalation fixes; there is no UNC prefix cache, no use-after-free, and no exploit primitive.
